paper_title: Provenance and Paleoclimate Analysis of Sandstone from Meluhu Formation in Kendari, Southeast Sulawesi

methods.description: Microscopic petrography was executed on 14 samples of Meluhu Sandstone to observe characteristics such as sorting, packing, compaction, degree of roundness, and mineral composition using the point counting method.

steps.description: Microscopic observation in thin sections was done at the laboratory of petrology-petrography, geological department, Institut Teknologi Sumatra.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microscopic observation of thin sections in a laboratory

steps.description: The petrographic method was used to observe the characteristics of the constituent components of 14 samples of sandstone.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze petrographic characteristics of sandstone samples

steps.description: Observed sandstone characteristics include sorting, packing, compaction, degree of roundness, and mineral composition using the point counting method.
steps.searchable_summary: use point counting method to analyze sandstone characteristics

methods.description: Identification of sandstone classification and determination of rock origin/provenance using various diagrams and methods.

steps.description: Identification of sandstone classification according to Pettijohn et al. (1987) based on the percentage of grain components, including quartz, feldspar, and lithic.
steps.searchable_summary: classify sandstone based on grain components percentage

steps.description: Determination of rock origin/provenance using a discriminant triangle diagram of quartz, feldspar, and lithic (QFL) from Dickinson et al. (1983).
steps.searchable_summary: determine rock provenance using QFL diagram

methods.description: Paleoclimate analysis using discriminant diagrams of quartz, feldspar, and rock fragment (QFR) and texture determination using Folk diagrams.

steps.description: Paleoclimate analysis used discriminant diagrams of quartz, feldspar, and rock fragment (QFR).
